channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral brings together 5 current and former US presidents to honor one of their own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they filed into the front pews at Washington National Cathedral, wearing dark suits and mostly solemn faces, five current and former presidents came together for Jimmy Carter's funeral.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Utah firefighters answer the call to battle Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: After a quick briefing, they hit the road to begin a 12-hour drive to California to battle the deadly and devastating Palisades Fire in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Inside Washington National Cathedral, the five men who've occupied the Oval Office since 1993 convened for a rare moment together at Jimmy Carter’s state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Ted Anthony, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Wildfires latest: Death toll expected to rise as fires rage through Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters are battling to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed five people, ravaged communities from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden administration wages last-minute battle over plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind
item.description: The Biden administration has doubled down on its unusual court battle to derail a plea deal that the government itself had reached with accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for his humility and service in Washington before his return to Georgia
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter was honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital before a second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's woodworking, painting and poetry reveal an introspective Renaissance Man
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — The world knew Jimmy Carter as a president and humanitarian, but he also was a woodworker, painter and poet, creating a body of artistic work that reflects deeply personal views of the global community — and himself.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app. Lemon8 resembles an amalgamation of the types of short-form videos found on TikTok and the picture-perfect aesthetic of Instagram and Pinterest.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles area fires
item.description: At least five people are dead and thousands of structures have been damaged or destroyed as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's rolls back diversity goals
item.description: Fast food giant McDonald’s has become the latest company to roll back diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) commitments following the Supreme Court’s 2023 decision ending affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: In Cincinnati, roller rink provides warmth and shelter from nation's Arctic blast
item.description: CINCINNATI (AP) — Tirrdell Byrd sat on the floor strumming his guitar as people milled about Over-The-Rhine Recreation Center, winter coats flung over folding chairs, belongings packed into shopping bags and wheeled totes, the smell of pizza lingering.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Peter Yarrow of Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: Yarrow, who also co-wrote the group's most enduring song, “Puff the Magic Dragon,” died Tuesday in New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Car abandoned on Colorado ski run; Driver blames GPS
item.description: A driver got a prime (but illegal) parking spot on the slopes Sunday night when their GPS led them right onto a ski run
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, Fox 5

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Donations received to fly migrant girl who lost legs on Mexican train and family
item.description: Inside the Humanitarian Respite Center on Monday a family of a little girl who lost her legs on a train coming to the border were preparing to leave the border after an outpour of donations were sent to help the family.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: CFPB finalizes rule barring medical debt from credit reports
item.description: The finalized rule will prevent medical bills from being included on credit reports used by lenders and will prohibit lenders from using medical information in their decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Immigration is a higher priority for Americans than it was a year ago, an AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More Americans say immigration should be a top focus for the U.S. government in 2025, as the country heads toward a new Republican administration in which President-elect Donald Trump has promised the mass deportations of migrants and an end to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, HUMERA LODHI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump Jr. plans Greenland visit after father stirs controversy
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. is planning to head to Greenland for a day-long trip just weeks after his father, President-elect Trump, said U.S. ownership of the Danish territory "is an absolute necessity.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: Court proceedings for man charged in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing are delayed several weeks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a New York City hotel is unlikely to appear in Manhattan federal court again before mid-February after prosecutors and lawyers on Monday requested more time to prepare the case for trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: After 2 years of shortfalls, California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit
item.description: TURLOCK, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's is the latest company to roll back diversity goals
item.description: Four years after launching a push for more diversity in its ranks, McDonald’s is ending some of its diversity practices, citing a U.S. Supreme Court decision that outlawed affirmative action in college admissions.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is HMPV? CDC 'monitoring' reports of spiking cases in China
item.description: The CDC’s actions come in response to China's government-run media confirming that positivity rates of HMPV hare rising in children under the age of 14, despite respiratory illnesses being down overall since last year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's launching new value menu: Here are the options
item.description: McDonald’s will launch a new “McValue platform” on Tuesday, aiming to give customers “even more ways to enjoy the food they love, for less," the fast-food giant says. The McValue menu, first announced in November, will include nationwide, local and app-based offers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5
